mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and declines by at least 0.5pp from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.81% as of 2008-09-01 indicates emergency easing underway
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle during financial stress
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 6% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% and increases by at least 0.3pp from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.1% as of 2008-09-01 reflects labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns exceeds -0.15 in fewer than 3 of 5 rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline below prior-year levels combined with falling housing starts indicates collapsing consumer demand and residential investment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 900 thousand units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 375473M and HOUST at 895K as of 2008-09-01 show demand weakness
regime_hint: demand shock with synchronized collapse in consumption and housing
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or interaction effect (measured as coefficient on RSAFS×HOUST interaction term) is not statistically significant at p<0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines below 110 index points signals contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and real economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward GDP expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 110.0 and declines by at least 0.5 points from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.3034 as of 2008-08-01 near critical threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines month-over-month combined with rising unemployment rate signals accelerating labor market deterioration that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137318K and UNRATE at 6.1% as of 2008-09-01 show dual labor weakness
regime_hint: synchronized labor market shock with job losses and rising unemployment
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p<0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 850 thousand units annualized signals residential investment collapse and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as construction and materials sectors face earnings headwinds.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 850 thousand units and declines by at least 50 units from prior month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 895K as of 2008-08-01 approaching critical support level
regime_hint: housing market collapse during financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is weaker than -0.12 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI remains elevated above 215 index points while federal funds rate falls below 2% creates real rate compression that initially supports equity valuations but signals monetary policy desperation, producing mixed but net-positive 20-day returns as liquidity effects dominate.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 2.0% AND CPIAUCSL > 215.0 AND (CPIAUCSL minus FEDFUNDS) > 213
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 218.88 and FEDFUNDS at 1.81% as of 2008-09-01
regime_hint: stagflationary shock with emergency monetary easing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL×FEDFUNDS) shows no statistically significant effect on forward returns at p<0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 remains below 12000 billion chained dollars indicates recessionary contraction in aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward across all sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 < 12000 billion chained 2017 dollars or GDPC1 declines from prior quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11727.4B as of 2008-04-01 indicates recession underway
regime_hint: demand-side recession with negative GDP growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to predict recession-period returns with directional accuracy exceeding 60%.
